_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Delaware County, IA. Record survival, courthouse history, and known losses. Manchester is the county seat. Delaware County has strong German Catholic settlement centered on Dyersville and New Vienna, and its records are intact with no major courthouse fire.

previous_courthouses.location: Delhi — original county seat, frame courthouse
previous_courthouses.fate: County seat moved to Manchester in 1880 after a contested election. Delhi served as county seat for nearly 40 years.
County seat transferred to Manchester. Records moved.
Replaced by current 1894 building. Records transferred.

record_losses.description: Delaware County has no known major courthouse fire or catastrophic record loss. The county seat contest between Delhi and Manchester (resolved in favor of Manchester in 1880) was contentious but did not result in record damage. Records transferred intact from Delhi to Manchester. The current 1894 courthouse has housed records continuously for over 130 years.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} filed a deed, marriage, or probate record in Delaware County, it almost certainly survives. The German Catholic community around Dyersville — one of the most cohesive immigrant communities in Iowa — left particularly rich church records that supplement the county archive.
record_losses.source: Merry, John F. History of Delaware County, Iowa. Chicago: S. J. Clarke Publishing, 1914.

deeds.completeness: Excellent — continuous from the territorial period. Land records from the earliest Euro-American settlement survive.
deeds.repository: Delaware County Recorder, Manchester; Iowa State Archives, Des Moines
deeds.online_access: FamilySearch microfilm; Ancestry.com Iowa land records

wills_estates.completeness: Good — probate records from early settlement survive
wills_estates.gaps: Few estates filed in the earliest years when population was small.
wills_estates.repository: Delaware County Clerk of Court, Manchester; Iowa State Archives

marriages.completeness: Good — civil marriage records from the territorial period
marriages.gaps: German Catholic marriages at Dyersville and New Vienna frequently recorded only in parish registers before 1880. The parish record may be the only documentation.
marriages.repository: Delaware County Recorder, Manchester; Iowa State Archives

court_minutes.completeness: Good — county and district court minutes from the territorial period
court_minutes.gaps: Justice of the peace records incomplete for some townships.
court_minutes.repository: Delaware County Courthouse, Manchester; Iowa State Archives

vital_records.completeness: Iowa required vital registration from 1880. German Catholic communities around Dyersville relied heavily on parish registration, and civil compliance was inconsistent until the 1900s. The Basilica of St. Francis Xavier in Dyersville maintained meticulous parish records that often exceed the detail of civil registrations.
vital_records.repository: Iowa Department of Public Health; Delaware County Recorder; Iowa State Archives

naturalization_records.completeness: Good — naturalization records important for the large German Catholic immigrant population around Dyersville and New Vienna, as well as Irish and other immigrant groups.
naturalization_records.gaps: Some immigrants naturalized in Dubuque County courts.
naturalization_records.repository: Delaware County District Court; National Archives — Kansas City; Iowa State Archives

substitute_sources.description: The Basilica of St. Francis Xavier in Dyersville (parish founded 1847) and St. Boniface in New Vienna maintained comprehensive sacramental registers in Latin and German. Dyersville became one of the largest German Catholic parishes in Iowa. The Basilica — designated a Minor Basilica in 1956 — maintains records dating to the 1840s.
substitute_sources.repository: Archdiocese of Dubuque Archives; Basilica of St. Francis Xavier, Dyersville; FamilySearch microfilm
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Critical for German Catholic vital events. Sacramental registers record baptisms with parents and godparents (often revealing kinship networks), marriages with parental consent and home parish, and burials with age and cause of death. Sponsors at baptism frequently indicate the social network within the parish.
substitute_sources.source: Archdiocese of Dubuque. Parish Histories and Registers. Merry, History of Delaware County.

substitute_sources.description: Federal population schedules 1850–1940 and Iowa state censuses (1856, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925).
substitute_sources.repository: National Archives; FamilySearch; Ancestry.com; Iowa State Archives
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Iowa's alternating federal and state censuses provide data approximately every five years. The 1856 state census captures the established German Catholic community at Dyersville before the Civil War.
substitute_sources.source: Iowa State Historical Society. Iowa Census Records Guide. Des Moines: ISHS, 2005.

substitute_sources.source_type: Delaware County plat books and atlases
substitute_sources.description: Published county atlases (1875, 1894, 1914) showing landowners by name. German Catholic surname clustering around Dyersville and New Vienna is immediately visible in the plat maps.
substitute_sources.repository: Delaware County Historical Society; Iowa State Historical Society; FamilySearch
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Plat maps reveal the geographic concentration of German Catholic families around the parish churches. Comparing editions shows the stability of these communities — many families remained on the same land for decades.
substitute_sources.source: Standard Atlas of Delaware County, Iowa. Chicago: Anderson Publishing, 1894.

narrative_hooks: Delaware County's records are intact from 1842 — the territorial period — and the German Catholic community around Dyersville left both civil and parish records that together provide one of the richest documentary legacies in eastern Iowa.

The Basilica of St. Francis Xavier at Dyersville — one of only a few basilicas in the United States outside a major city — maintained parish registers that may record {ancestor_name}'s baptism, marriage, and burial with a detail that exceeds any civil record.

The county seat contest between Delhi and Manchester was bitter, but the records survived the move intact — if {ancestor_name} transacted business at the Delhi courthouse before 1880, the deed or court record is now in Manchester.

If {ancestor_name} was part of the Dyersville German Catholic community, the plat maps tell a story of remarkable stability — the same surnames on the same quarter-sections across forty years of atlas editions.
source_references: Merry, John F. History of Delaware County, Iowa, and Its People. Chicago: S. J. Clarke Publishing, 1914.
Archdiocese of Dubuque. Parish Histories and Registers. Dubuque: Archdiocese of Dubuque Archives.
Iowa State Historical Society. Iowa Census Records Guide. Des Moines: ISHS, 2005.
Standard Atlas of Delaware County, Iowa. Chicago: Anderson Publishing, 1894.
